log.decision: Use OpenClaw workspace-file architecture for agent identity and behavior
log.reasoning: Workspace files (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, HEARTBEAT.md) give the agent persistent, inspectable identity without custom code. Fully auditable.

log.decision: Store deposits in deposits.json as plaintext; encryption handled by TEE enclave at deploy
log.reasoning: Pre-TEE development needs a working system. Encryption at the enclave boundary is the right architecture — the TEE seals the key on first boot, never exports it.

log.decision: Use direct Telegram Bot API via curl instead of openclaw message send for delivery
log.reasoning: openclaw message send has a known bug with DM thread IDs when sending proactively. Direct Bot API curl is reliable and deterministic.

log.decision: Use himalaya CLI for email delivery instead of SMTP skill
log.reasoning: himalaya is already configured with charonbot30@gmail.com and supports file attachments cleanly. Simpler than wiring a raw SMTP skill.

log.input: Name: Charon, emoji: ⚰️, vibe: ferryman, ancient, reliable
Tone: calm, certain, direct. Always show times in IST. Never one-word answers to real questions.
Intake flow: Receive → WHEN → HOW → Write to disk. Check-in handling. Delivery via Telegram/email/onchain. Hard rules.
deposits.json schema, shell helpers, himalaya email commands, Telegram Bot API curl, onchain via cast
Every 5 min: read deposits.json, check conditions, fire delivery, update status
heartbeat.every: 1m, target: last, Telegram bot token, model: nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5
capgoblin/charon_agent:latest — OpenClaw + workspace files packaged for EigenCompute
Registered Charon as ERC-8004 agent on Base Mainnet via The Synthesis hackathon API
Published Charon announcement on Moltbook with project description and repo link
